title: Pithiviers
subtitle: Two rounds of puff pastry enclosing a rich almond cream
excerpt: Two rounds of puff pastry sandwich a dome of frangipane made from ground almonds, butter, eggs, and sugar. The edges are crimped and the top is scored in a curved spiral before baking. The pastry puffs around the dense almond centre. Named after the town of Pithiviers in the Loiret region of France, where it has been a regional speciality since at least the 17th century.
sourceNotes: The pithiviers is documented as a regional speciality of Pithiviers in the Loiret since at least the 17th century. The almond cream filling (crème d'amandes) is a standard of French pâtisserie from the 18th century onward. In its Galette des Rois form, a charm is hidden inside and served on the feast of Epiphany. The pithiviers reached Britain through French bistro cooking in the 1980s and 1990s. The recipe here is the classic pâtisserie version with two pastry rounds, almond filling, and spiral scoring, not the later British adaptation using a vegetable filling.

recipe.freezeNotes: Freeze the assembled, unbaked pithiviers on a lined tray for up to 1 month. Bake from frozen, adding 10 minutes to the bake time.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The frangipane can be made 3 days ahead and refrigerated. Assemble and chill the pithiviers for at least 30 minutes before baking for a cleaner puff.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: conventional oven. Fan at 180°C. The pastry should be deeply golden and crisp. The almond filling causes the sides to bow outward as the pastry bakes; this is expected and gives the characteristic profile.

recipeTools.notes: large baking tray, lined with baking paper

glossaryTerms.definition: A filling of ground almonds, butter, eggs, and sugar beaten together until smooth. In strict French pâtisserie, frangipane refers to a mixture of crème pâtissière and crème d'amandes. In British baking the term is used for the almond-butter-egg-sugar mixture alone, as here and in Bakewell tart. Frangipane puffs slightly when baked, setting to a moist, macaroon-like interior. It can be flavoured with almond extract, rum, or citrus zest.

content.text: The pithiviers is assembled from two rounds of puff pastry enclosing a dome of

. Chill the assembled tart for at least 30 minutes before baking: cold butter in the pastry and a firm filling give a cleaner puff and a more defined spiral score. The spiral is cut from the centre outward in a curved motion with a sharp knife, going only halfway through the pastry so the filling stays sealed inside.

Beat the butter and caster sugar together until light and pale. Beat in the eggs one at a time. Fold in the ground almonds, flour, and almond extract until smooth. Refrigerate the frangipane for at least 30 minutes.

Roll the chilled puff pastry to 4 mm thickness and cut two rounds: one 22 cm and one 24 cm. Place the smaller round on a lined baking tray. Mound the frangipane in the centre, leaving a 3 cm border bare. Brush the border with egg wash. Lay the larger round on top and press the edges firmly to seal. Crimp the edge with a fork. Brush the top with egg wash, avoiding the crimped edge. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.

Heat the oven to 200°C conventional (180°C fan). Using the tip of a sharp knife, score a curved spiral from the centre to the edge of the top pastry, cutting only halfway through. Make a small steam hole in the centre. Bake for 28 to 32 minutes until deeply golden and crisp. Dust with icing sugar and return to the oven for 2 minutes to glaze the top to a shine. Cool on a rack for at least 20 minutes before cutting.
